model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: Houston enters this Round of 32 matchup with a clear statistical advantage, ranking fifth in both KenPom and Torvik compared to Saint Mary's standing at 24th. The Cougars boast an elite defense that ranks fourth nationally, which should be able to stifle a Gaels offense that ranks outside the top 40. While Saint Mary's features a stout defense of their own and prefers a methodical pace that could limit possessions, Houston's superior offensive execution and physical style of play give them the necessary edge. With a 10-point advantage in adjusted efficiency margin, Houston's overwhelming talent and balanced profile make them the confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Houston vs Saint Mary's Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Houston holds a significant statistical advantage over Saint Mary's, ranking 5th in KenPom compared to the Gaels' 24th. Houston's elite defense and superior offense should be enough to overcome Saint Mary's methodical pace.
evidence: Houston is ranked #5 in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of 33.55, while Saint Mary's is ranked #24 with a margin of 23.11.
Houston's defense ranks #4 nationally, which should stifle Saint Mary's #43 ranked offense.

result.title: Houston's Terrance Arceneaux suffers apparent Achilles injury
result.snippet: Houston Cougars coach Kelvin Sampson believes guard Terrance Arceneaux suffered a torn Achilles in his team's 70-66 win over Texas A&M on Saturday.

result.title: Houston's Terrance Arceneaux has Achilles tear, out for season
result.snippet: The 6-foot-6 Arceneaux suffered the injury in the Cougars' victory over Texas A&M on Saturday, when he was helped to the locker room and returned to the bench with a boot on his right foot.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
